meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Can't Debug Code
content.seo_description: Learn how to use analytics to solve debugging blocks. A beginner's guide to tracking app state using Antigravity and Claude Code to master software engineering.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug code, you are likely flying blind. By implementing event-based analytics, you turn silent failures into observable data streams. Tracking state changes and user interactions reveals the exact moment your logic breaks, transforming 'unsolvable' bugs into clear, actionable logs that lead directly to the culprit.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your code refuses to behave. You have spent hours staring at a logic block that seems perfect, yet the output remains fundamentally broken. This is the 'debugging wall'—a rite of passage for every engineer, but one that stops most students in their tracks. You don't need more intuition; you need visibility.

True mastery isn't about writing bug-free code; it's about building systems that tell you exactly why they failed. By integrating telemetry and analytics into your application, you stop guessing and start diagnosing. This guide will show you how to leverage advanced tooling to turn your application into an open book, giving you the confidence to ship faster and learn deeper.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path
steps.description: Identify the primary user flow in your app. Before tracking errors, you must understand what 'success' looks like in terms of state changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the 3 most critical state variables that dictate the success or failure of the main user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify critical state variables for user flow tracking'

steps.title: Instrument Event Emitters
steps.description: Place trace points at the entry and exit of your core functions to capture the 'before' and 'after' state of your data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a wrapper function for my core logic that logs the input parameters and return values to a structured analytics object.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file main.py --add 'Implement structured telemetry wrapper for core functions'

steps.title: Capture Contextual Error Snapshots
steps.description: Don't just log an error; log the entire application state when the exception triggers to make debugging reproducible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a global exception handler that captures the current application state and dumps it to a local JSON file whenever a crash occurs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file error_handler.py --add 'Implement state snapshotting on exception'

steps.title: Visualize the Data Flow
steps.description: Use your new analytics logs to map out the sequence of operations, identifying where the data diverges from your expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my log files and generate a sequence diagram showing the flow of data and where the unexpected state change occurs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate sequence diagram based on log files'

steps.title: Automate Regression Checks
steps.description: Once a bug is found and fixed, use your analytics to build a test case that ensures the error never resurfaces.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unit test based on the specific telemetry data that revealed the previous bug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create automated regression test based on telemetry snapshot'

pro_tips: Always log the 'previous state' alongside the 'current state' to catch mutation errors.
Use unique correlation IDs for every user session to isolate bugs that only happen to specific inputs.
Keep your logs structured (JSON) rather than plain text to make them searchable via CLI tools.
Never log sensitive user data like passwords or tokens; sanitize your logs before they hit the disk.
If you can't reproduce a bug, use your analytics to simulate the exact sequence of events that led to the failure.
content.next_steps: Now that you have visibility, begin building a dashboard to monitor these events in real-time. This will move you from 'debugging' to 'proactive maintenance'.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. With these tools, you are no longer a struggling student but an engineer with a blueprint for success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
